Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: It is present via transgenic expression in the purple tomato line used in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Not explicitly stated in the text provided.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The Results support transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes by Del/Ros1.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study directly demonstrates stacking Ros1/Del with AtMYB12 to produce Indigo fruit with higher anthocyanins.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Nuclear localization is not stated or tested in this paper.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Anthocyanin biosynthesis is directly the process controlled in this study.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The purple transgenic line expresses Ros1 in fruit, evidenced by phenotype and RT-qPCR.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The comparisons are in ripening fruit/post-breaker stages.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Ros1 promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit with Del and further in Indigo with AtMYB12.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR and metabolite profiling are directly presented.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Pigmentation is explicit, but antioxidant/nutritional value is not directly tested here.

corrections.old_value: Endogenous transgenes in purple tomato line
corrections.reason: The paper refers to the purple Del/Ros1 line but does not specify promoter(s) for Ros1 in this text.
Nuclear localization is not stated or tested in this paper.
